mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate growing consumer demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows a rising trend but SPX 20-day returns are negative in >60% of months, or rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 6.5%, it signals labor market weakness and falling consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns after UNRATE crosses 6.5% are positive in more than 50% of cases, or if average forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) reinforce the signal of robust economic expansion, leading to strong positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day quarter as both income and consumption grow.
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces negative average forward returns or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by rising federal funds rate, the cost of capital increases, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over short horizons of 5 trading days as markets adjust to higher rates.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day returns are positive in >50% of tightening episodes as defined, or if rank IC for FEDFUNDS direction relative to returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals increasing manufacturing output and capital expenditure, which boosts overall economic activity and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises but SPX 20-day returns decline in more than half of instances, or rank IC < 0 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 4%, it triggers fears of Fed tightening and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are not significantly negative after threshold breaches, or if rank IC for CPI direction is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is rising while the fed funds rate remains stable (no change >0.25% over 3 months), it indicates a non-inflationary growth environment that is favorable for equities over a quarter horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint condition fails to produce positive average forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In the early expansion phase of the business cycle, strong GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% leads to above-trend earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon, but this mechanism reverses in late-cycle when overheating concerns dominate.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are not consistently positive in early expansion, or if the mechanism fails to exhibit regime-dependent reversal in late-cycle periods.
